message:
Fix 14 correctness and safety issues across inference, collection, redaction, and telemetry subsystems

pt-config:
- Respect case_insensitive flag in regex and glob fallback pattern
  matching; strip ^ and $ anchors from regex fallback patterns

pt-core (action):
- Replace unsafe libc::getpwuid() with /etc/passwd file parsing
  for thread-safe UID-to-username resolution
- Fix launchctl service status detection to parse plist dictionary
  output (search for "PID" = key) instead of assuming TSV format

pt-core (collect):
- Extract systemd unit info from name=systemd cgroup v1 controller
- Query systemctl show with unit name directly instead of unreliable
  --pid flag; remove double-fallback; update tests to pass cgroup
  unit name

pt-core (decision):
- Move selected_ids/not_selected computation inside local_search
  loop to prevent stale candidate sets across iterations

pt-core (inference):
- Fix BOCPD step counter: increment self.step before early returns
  in single-observation and outlier code paths
- Extract actual prior from "prior" evidence term in posterior
  instead of hardcoded uniform [0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25]
- Fix ImpactScorer child count: return Some(0) for missing PIDs
  when cache is populated instead of None (which triggers wasteful
  /proc fallback scans)

pt-core (supervision):
- Use bounds-checked .get() instead of direct slice indexing when
  parsing /proc/pid/stat to prevent panics on malformed entries

pt-redact:
- Preserve text before temp session directory in canonicalization;
  use .replace() for /tmp/ and /var/tmp/ to handle mid-path occurrences
- Use .chars().count() instead of .len() in truncate_value() for
  correct Unicode multi-byte string handling

pt-redact (hash):
- Write key material to .tmp file first, then atomically rename
  to prevent partial/corrupt key files on interrupted writes

pt-telemetry:
- Close writer and atomically rename temp-to-output in
  BatchedWriter Drop to prevent orphaned temp files

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
